column1: Token Math Template - Candidate Work Area

column2: This system runs eight workflow stages over a 750-account B2B SaaS portfolio: a daily memory/retrieval and account-health scan (Haiku + embeddings) feeds a prioritization tier (Urgent/Watch/Expansion/Stable) that drives three downstream consumers -- inbound ticket routing, customer check-in briefing, and biweekly targeted intervention planning -- each using Claude Sonnet 4.6 for the judgment those tasks require. Junior-drafted customer-facing outputs are checked weekly against named quality standards (GPT-5.4 mini), and a final escalation/responsiveness stage (Claude Opus 4.7) consolidates every flagged ticket and quality-review miss into escalation, follow-up, or immediate-resolution. Per-call token sizes are measured from a runnable simulation (cs_workflow_simulation/) on an 18-account sample dataset; the rows below scale those measured per-run costs to the assignment's 750-account / 30-tickets-per-week / 12-check-ins-per-week production volumes.

=IF(B5<0.25,"Low utilization: explain why efficient or under-scoped",IF(B5<0.6,"Conservative: explain tradeoffs",IF(B5<0.9,"Substantial production usage: justify assumptions",IF(B5<1.1,"Near budget: explain risk controls","Over budget: optimize or justify"))))

column22: Review flag

column20: MEASURED tokens/cost per run (token_cost_summary.csv, 18-account sample). SCALED ESTIMATE: volume = 750 accounts/business day per assignment's stated portfolio size. ASSUMPTION: 2% retry (embeddings rarely fail), 1.0x QA (no human QA on retrieval itself).

column20: NOT MEASURED -- FULL ESTIMATE, all three columns. The 4 evaluation checks in workflow.py (Urgent coverage, high-severity routing, brief completeness, full QS coverage) are deterministic Python and cost $0 in tokens today. This row estimates an LLM-as-judge audit of a 10% sample of weekly quality-review volume (333 x 10% = ~33); token sizes assumed by analogy to the quality-review stage, not measured from any run.
